name: mobile-testing
description: Write and run tests for React Native apps using Jest and React Native Testing Library. Use when creating tests, debugging failures, or setting up test infrastructure.
capabilities: Provides component test templates using React Native Testing Library
Provides hook test templates with renderHook and act utilities
Provides utility test templates for pure functions
Shows mocking patterns for external modules, databases, and navigation
Includes async testing patterns and form testing examples
Lists troubleshooting tips for common testing issues
author: BabakBar
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown templates and guidance for React Native testing. No executable code, network operations, or file system operations beyond reading its own content. Static findings are false positives from the scanner misinterpreting documentation code blocks and metadata as executable security risks.
valueStatement: Writing tests for React Native applications is time-consuming without proper templates and patterns. This skill provides ready-to-use testing templates for components, hooks, and utilities along with mocking patterns and best practices.
userTitle: Test React Native apps with Jest
limitations: Does not execute tests or verify test results
Does not configure Jest or testing infrastructure automatically
Does not generate test coverage reports
Does not integrate with CI/CD pipelines

useCases.title: Writing unit tests
useCases.description: Get templates for testing components, hooks, and utility functions in React Native apps

useCases.title: Creating integration tests
useCases.description: Set up integration tests for features with proper mocking of external dependencies

useCases.title: Improving test coverage
useCases.description: Follow best practices to increase test coverage with isolated, maintainable tests

promptTemplates.title: Component test
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Jest test for MyButton component that renders correctly and calls onPress when pressed

promptTemplates.title: Hook test
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a test for useCounter hook that increments and decrements the count value

promptTemplates.title: Mock external
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to mock the database module in Jest tests for a React Native app

promptTemplates.title: Async test
promptTemplates.prompt: Write an async test that waits for data to load and verifies the rendered content

bestPractices: Test user-facing behavior instead of internal implementation details
Use testID props for element selection instead of fragile text queries
Mock external dependencies to keep tests fast and isolated
antiPatterns: Testing implementation details instead of user behavior
Using text queries that break with UI changes
Skipping mocking of external services causing flaky tests

faq.question: Which React Native versions are supported?
faq.answer: Works with React Native 0.70+ and Expo SDK 50+. Compatible with Jest 28+ and React Native Testing Library.

faq.question: What is the maximum test file size?
faq.answer: No enforced limit. Keep tests focused with one concern per file. Large components may need multiple test files.

faq.question: Can this skill integrate with CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidance for running Jest in CI. Add npm test to your CI pipeline configuration.

faq.question: Is test data stored securely?
faq.answer: Tests run locally. No test data leaves your machine. Jest stores coverage reports locally by default.

faq.question: Why are my tests timing out?
faq.answer: Increase Jest timeout, check for unresolved promises, use waitFor or findBy queries for async content.

faq.question: How is this different from Detox for E2E testing?
faq.answer: Jest with RNTL tests units and components in isolation. Detox tests full app flow on simulator. Use both together.
